operating_state: Revenue reached $30.0 billion, up 122% year-over-year and 15% sequentially, driven by Data Center growth of 154% annually from exceptional Hopper GPU platform demand across cloud service providers, enterprise customers, and sovereign AI initiatives. Gaming revenue grew on GeForce RTX 40 Series strength, while Professional Visualization and Automotive segments posted double-digit growth.
cost_structure: Gross margin decreased sequentially to 75.1% from 78.4% but increased 5.0 percentage points year-over-year, with strong Data Center revenue mix offset by $908 million in inventory provisions for low-yielding Blackwell material creating a 2.7% margin headwind. Operating expenses increased 48% annually driven by compensation growth reflecting employee expansion and compute infrastructure investments supporting accelerated product development cycles.
material_events: Company executed Blackwell GPU mask changes to improve production yield with production ramp scheduled for Q4 fiscal 2025 and several billion dollars in Blackwell revenue expected that quarter. Board approved additional $50.0 billion share repurchase authorization bringing total available to $53.9 billion, with $15.1 billion repurchased in first half of fiscal 2025. Operations transitioned certain activities out of China and Hong Kong following regulatory requirements.
macro_exposures: Export control restrictions significantly expanded with new licensing requirements for products exceeding performance thresholds destined for China, Middle East, and other regions, with no licenses received for restricted product shipments to China. Data Center revenue in China grew sequentially but remains below pre-October 2023 control levels. Geopolitical conflict in Israel affects approximately 4,000 employees primarily supporting networking product development.
forward_outlook: Hopper demand remains strong with shipments expected to increase in second half of fiscal 2025, while management committed to annual Data Center architecture refresh cadence with greater product variety. Company continues increasing supply capacity with existing and new suppliers through prepaid manufacturing agreements to support demand projections and supply chain diversification.
uncertainty_profile: Blackwell production yield improvements and ramp timing present execution risk with potential for continued inventory provisions impacting margins as the company manages accelerated annual architecture cadence. Export control rules remain subject to frequent changes with unclear licensing standards potentially disadvantaging NVIDIA against competitors, while pending regulatory compliance creates cost uncertainties.
